info.title: VTP Automation Interface
info.description:
This is the OpenAPI documentation of the VTP Automation Interface.

Depending on the installed applications, you can select different API definitions.

Using an OpenAPI generator, you can generate the client code for the programming language that best suits your automation environment.

get.description: Returns all VTP applications installed on the device in a JSON string.

post.description: Installs a VTP application. Expects the VTP application as binary content in the requests body.

get.description:
Gets a specific VTP application by its identifier. Expects the parameter {id} as string in the query.
            If the identifier is not known, query for all applications and look for your desired applications displayed parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Represents the identifier of a VTP application.

delete.description:
Deletes a specific VTP application by its identifier. Expects the parameter {id} as string in the query.
            If the identifier is not known, query for all applications and look for your desired applications displayed parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Represents the identifier of a VTP application.

put.description:
Enables a specific VTP application by its identifier. Expects the parameter {id} as string in the query.
            If the identifier is not known, query for all applications and look for your desired applications displayed parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Represents the identifier of a VTP application.

put.description:
Disables a specific VTP application by its identifier. Expects the parameter {id} as string in the query.
            If the identifier is not known, query for all applications and look for your desired applications displayed parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Represents the identifier of a VTP application.

put.description:
Starts a specific VTP application by its identifier. Expects the parameter {id} as string in the query and its alias in the header of the request.
            If the identifier or alias is not known, query for all applications and look for your desired applications displayed parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Represents the identifier of a VTP application.

parameters.name: alias
parameters.description: Represents the alias of a VTP application.

put.description:
Stops a specific VTP application by its identifier. Expects the parameter {id} as string in the query and its alias in the header of the request.
            If the identifier or alias is not known, query for all applications and look for your desired applications displayed parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Represents the identifier of a VTP application.

parameters.name: alias
parameters.description: Represents the alias of a VTP application.

get.summary: Gets a list of all certificates of a specific store.
get.description: Gets a list of all certificates of a specific store. Expects the certificate stores name as parameter {store} as string in the query.

parameters.name: store
parameters.description: Represents the name of the certificate store.

delete.summary: Removes a certificate from a certificate store.
delete.description:
Remove the certificate identified by header parameters. Expects the certificate stores name as parameter {store} as string in the query.
            Expects the values for Hash, Subject, Issuer, NotBefore, NotAfter in the requests header as string parameters.

parameters.name: store
parameters.description: Represents the name of the certificate store.

parameters.name: Hash
parameters.description: Hash of the certificate, formatted as string.

parameters.name: Subject
parameters.description: Subject of the certificate, formatted as string.

parameters.name: Issuer
parameters.description: Issuer of the certificate, formatted as string.

parameters.name: NotBefore
parameters.description: Validation time of the certificate, string in DateTime format, e.g. "2028-08-01T23:59:59".

parameters.name: NotAfter
parameters.description: Validation time of the certificate, string in DateTime format, e.g. "2028-08-01T23:59:59".

post.summary: Adds a certificate to a certificate store.
post.description:
Adds a certificate to a certificate store. Expects the certificate stores name as parameter {store} as string in the query.
            Expects the certificates name in the requests header as string parameter.
            Expects the certificate as binary content in the requests body.

parameters.name: store
parameters.description: Represents the name of the certificate store.

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: Represents the name including file extension of the certificate.

get.summary: Gets a certificate in Base64 format.
get.description:
Gets a certificate identified by header parameters in Base64 format. Expects the certificate stores name as parameter {store} as string in the query.
            Expects the values for Hash, Subject, Issuer, NotBefore, NotAfter in the requests header as string parameters.

parameters.name: store
parameters.description: Represents the name of the certificate store.

parameters.name: Hash
parameters.description: Hash of the certificate, formatted as string.

parameters.name: Subject
parameters.description: Subject of the certificate, formatted as string.

parameters.name: Issuer
parameters.description: Issuer of the certificate, formatted as string.

parameters.name: NotBefore
parameters.description: Validation time of the certificate, string in DateTime format, e.g. "2028-08-01T23:59:59".

parameters.name: NotAfter
parameters.description: Validation time of the certificate, string in DateTime format, e.g. "2028-08-01T23:59:59".

get.description: Gets the current channel configuration of the device. Returns the file stream that can be written to disk as a *.vtpcc file containing the configuration in JSON format.

put.description:
Imports a channel configuration to the device.
            Expects a valid *.vtpcc file as binary content in the requests body.

get.summary: Gets the USB driver synchronization state.
get.description: Gets the current state of the USB driver synchronization of the device.

200.description: JSON string formatted USB driver synchronization state.

put.summary: Sets the state of the USB driver synchronization.
put.description:
Sets the current state of the USB driver synchronization of the device.
            Expects the state as Boolean parameter in the query.

200.description: JSON string formatted USB driver synchronization state.

get.summary: Gets the date and time settings.
get.description: Gets the date and time settings of the device, formatted as JSON string.

200.description: JSON string formatted date and time settings.

put.summary: Sets the date and time settings.
put.description:
Sets the date and time settings of the device to the settings provided in the requests body.
            Expects the settings in JSON format, analog to the response of the corresponding GET request. Expects at least a valid entry for dateTime and timeZoneInfo.

get.description: Gets the free and used space of the devices hard disk.

200.description: Hard disk information, formatted as JSON string

get.description: Gets the VTP version installed on the device.

200.description: Version information, formatted as JSON string

get.description: Gets the image information of the image installed on the device.

200.description: Image information, formatted as JSON string

get.description: Gets the Operating System (OS) of the device.

200.description: OS information, formatted as JSON string

get.description: Gets the host name of the device.
Gets the article number of the device.
Gets the serial number of the device.
Gets the device description set by the user.

get.summary: Gets the date and time settings.
get.description: Gets the date and time settings of the device, formatted as JSON string.

200.description: JSON string formatted date and time settings.

get.description: Gets the status of ERT on the device, formatted as JSON string.
Gets a Vector support report for the device. Returns the report as stream that can be written to disk as a *.vsupportrep file.

200.description: Stream containing the Vector support report

get.description: Gets the status of ERT on the device, formatted as JSON string.

put.description:
Activates or deactivates ERT on the device.
            Expects the desired state as Boolean in the requests query.

get.summary: Gets a list of all network adapters.
get.description: Gets a list containing information on all network adapters of the device formatted as JSON string.

200.description: List of all network adapters, formatted as JSON string

put.description:
Configure a specific network adapter on the device.
            Expects the network adapters parameters in the requests body, formatted as JSON string, analog to the response of a GET query.

get.description:
Gets information of a specific network adapter on the device.
            Expects the name of the network adapter in the requests query.

200.description: Network adapter information, formatted as JSON string

hash.description: Hash of the certificate, formatted as string.

subject.description: Subject of the certificate, formatted as string.

issuer.description: Issuer of the certificate, formatted as string.

notBefore.description: Validation time of the certificate, string in DateTime format, e.g. "2028-08-01T23:59:59".

notAfter.description: Validation time of the certificate, string in DateTime format, e.g. "2028-08-01T23:59:59".

CertificateModel.description: Provides information about a certificate. Also used as identifier for a certificate.

MemoryModel.description: Provides information about the memory usage of a specific partition (free/used).
